name: screenshots
context: Capture full-page, viewport, element, and visual-regression screenshots with stable viewport, masks, traces, and artifact paths.
info: Playwright screenshots are both an automation artifact and a test assertion surface. This skill covers page screenshots, element screenshots, full-page capture, deterministic viewport setup, masking dynamic content, visual comparison with toHaveScreenshot, artifact organization, and when to use screenshots for research versus CI gates. Agents use it to inspect rendered pages, document UI state, verify generated frontend changes, compare visual output, or attach evidence to debugging reports.
body:
# Playwright Screenshots

Use screenshots as evidence for browser research and as assertions for visual regression tests.

```bash
nodes/playwright/scripts/browser-capture.mjs https://example.com \
  --screenshot artifacts/example.png \
  --viewport 1440x900 \
  --full-page

nodes/playwright/scripts/browser-capture.mjs https://example.com \
  --selector "main" \
  --screenshot artifacts/main.png
```

```typescript
await page.screenshot({ path: 'artifacts/page.png', fullPage: true });
await page.locator('[data-testid=chart]').screenshot({ path: 'artifacts/chart.png' });
await expect(page).toHaveScreenshot('dashboard.png', {
  fullPage: true,
  mask: [page.locator('[data-testid=clock]')]
});
```

Keep viewport, browser, locale, timezone, color scheme, and fonts stable when screenshot diffs matter.

sections.title: Capture modes
sections.body: `page.screenshot()` captures the viewport by default and the full scrollable document with `{ fullPage: true }`. `locator.screenshot()` captures one element. The helper script writes a screenshot and optional HTML/text summary so an agent can inspect both pixels and page content.

sections.title: Visual assertions
sections.body: Use `expect(page).toHaveScreenshot(name)` or `expect(locator).toHaveScreenshot(name)` in `@playwright/test`. First run creates snapshots; later runs compare pixels. Use `--update-snapshots` only when the change is intentional. Store snapshots in git if they are part of the UI contract.

sections.title: Stabilization
sections.body: Fix viewport and browser project, wait for the real ready condition, mask clocks or avatars, disable animations when the app supports it, seed test data, and avoid screenshots while network activity is still mutating the page. For research captures, save HTML and text alongside the image so the screenshot can be interpreted later.

sections.title: Artifact policy
sections.body: Screenshots of authenticated or private pages can contain secrets and personal data. Store them under an ignored `artifacts/` or CI artifact retention policy. Before attaching to issues or pull requests, review the image and any paired HTML for tokens, user data, or internal URLs.

links.context: Use traces and videos when screenshots are not enough
Use visual assertions in test suites
CSS and layout changes often need visual verification

related.note: The browser capture script uses Playwright screenshot APIs.
Visual assertions are part of Playwright test suites.
Traces include screenshots and DOM snapshots around actions.

scripts.name: Capture Screenshot
scripts.description: Open a page and save a full-page, viewport, or selector screenshot with optional HTML and text artifacts.

depends.reason: Screenshot capture requires installed browser binaries.

frontmatter.author: dojo-community
frontmatter.audience: frontend developers, QA engineers, AI agents
